crystals.name: Selenite
crystals.subtitle: The Stone of Clarity
crystals.why: Its luminous calm makes it a stone for clearing and connecting.
crystals.how_to_use: Place it where you sit, or hold it to reset.
crystals.desc: With its pale, luminous look, selenite is often used to clear the mind and open to something higher. Many place it nearby as a reminder to set the day aside and tune in.
crystals.care_note: Selenite is soft and water-sensitive — keep it dry and cleanse with smoke, sound, or moonlight instead of water.

m1_quick: Amethyst, lapis lazuli, labradorite, selenite, clear quartz, herkimer diamond, and kyanite are among the most commonly carried crystals for spiritual growth. Many people hold one as a cue to settle the surface and listen for something deeper. Crystals are complementary wellness tools; a spiritual path is walked through practice and reflection, not granted by a stone.
m2_understanding: A spiritual life means different things to different people — a sense of connection, a search for meaning, a practice of turning inward. What it shares is the willingness to look past the surface. Most of that work is quiet and slow, and a small, tangible cue can mark it: a stone held during reflection, a piece kept where you sit, a reminder that this stretch of time is set aside for listening rather than doing.
m25_chose: We chose these seven because they speak to the most common spiritual intentions in crystal practice: connection, inner vision, exploration, amplifying intention, attunement, and steady practice. Their meanings come from crystal tradition and personal practice, not clinical evidence.
m5_use: Hold it in practice. A stone in the palm during meditation or reflection.
Keep it where you sit. On a shelf or bedside, as a cue to tune in.
Carry it through the day. A pocket stone as a reminder of the path.
Pair it with a practice. A few breaths, a question held in silence.
m6_combos: Pairing is about layering intentions. A popular combination is Amethyst + Selenite — connection paired with clearing, often kept together where you sit. Lapis Lazuli + Labradorite is another, chosen for inner vision and exploration.
m8_intro: Explore genuine natural crystal pieces chosen for the inner path — stones, bracelets, and forms you can hold or keep nearby, spanning the seven crystals above.
closing: A spiritual path is walked, not arrived at — and a real stone can be a steady cue to pause and listen inward. Start with one and let practice carry the rest. For more, explore Spiritual Connection.

faq.q: Can crystals make me more spiritual?
faq.a: Not on their own. Crystals can't grant spirituality. Many people use them as tangible cues to pause and turn inward; a spiritual life is built through practice and reflection, not a stone.

faq.q: Are crystals scientifically proven to aid spiritual growth?
faq.a: No. There is no clinical evidence that crystals aid spiritual growth. Any felt benefit usually comes from the ritual of pausing and reflecting, not a proven effect of the stone.

faq.q: Which crystal is best for spiritual growth and connection?
faq.a: Amethyst is the most commonly chosen — long tied to spiritual awareness and a calm, listening stance.

faq.q: What is the best crystal for meditation and inner vision?
faq.a: Lapis lazuli and labradorite — lapis for seeking deeper truth, labradorite for exploring inner realms.

faq.q: Which crystal helps me clear my mind for spiritual practice?
faq.a: Selenite is often used to clear the mind and open to something higher.

faq.q: How should I cleanse spiritual crystals?
faq.a: Smoke (sage, palo santo), sound (singing bowl), or moonlight work for most stones. Note that selenite is water-sensitive — keep it dry and avoid water cleansing.

faq.q: What is the best crystal bracelet for spiritual growth?
faq.a: An amethyst bracelet is the most common first choice — durable, widely available, and easy to wear daily as a cue to turn inward.
